question_title: What will US airline passenger volume be for these weeks in April 2026, according to the TSA? (Apr 6–12, 2026)
summary:
This question asks for total TSA passenger volume for April 6-12, 2026 (Mon-Sun). Four of seven days are already known from TSA data: Mon 2,705,640 + Tue 2,207,180 + Wed 2,278,770 + Thu 2,691,308 = 9,882,898. The remaining three days (Fri-Sun, Apr 10-12) must be estimated.

Using day-of-week ratio analysis from 5 recent weeks (excluding Easter/Good Friday anomalies from week 5), I estimate Fri ~2.78M, Sat ~2.77M, Sun ~2.50M, for a total of ~17.93M. This aligns with YoY comparisons showing 2026 Mon-Thu volumes are 2.5% above 2025 equivalents, suggesting the jet fuel crisis has not materially reduced passenger demand despite United Airlines cutting ~5% of routes. Multiple estimation methods (ratio-based, DOW averages, YoY from 2025 and 2024) all converge in the 17.6M-17.9M range. The distribution is tight because 57% of the total is already locked in as actual data.

factors.description: 4 of 7 days already known (Mon-Thu Apr 6-9): 9,882,898 passengers, fixing 57% of the total
Day-of-week ratio analysis from recent weeks (excl. Easter effects) estimates Fri ~2.78M, Sat ~2.77M, Sun ~2.50M
YoY comparison: 2026 Mon-Thu is 2.5% above 2025 equivalent, volumes healthy despite jet fuel crisis
Jet fuel crisis and 5% route cuts by United - impact already captured in Mon-Thu actuals which are UP year-over-year
No holiday effects this week (Easter was Apr 5), normal travel patterns expected for Fri-Sun

reasoning:
**Forecast: ~17.92M passengers (90% CI: 17.65M – 18.16M)**

Key findings:
- **4 of 7 days known**: Mon-Thu Apr 6-9 totals 9,882,898 (from TSA data)
- **3 days to estimate**: Fri-Sun Apr 10-12, estimated at ~8.05M combined based on day-of-week patterns from recent weeks
- **Jet fuel crisis effect**: Despite the headline about rising fuel costs and route cuts, actual Mon-Thu volumes are **2.5% above** 2025 equivalents — the crisis hasn't reduced passenger demand yet
- **Easter adjustment**: Week 5 (Mar 30-Apr 5) showed low Fri/Sat due to Good Friday and Easter Saturday, but this week has no holiday effects, so I excluded those anomalous data points from the ratio estimation
- **Distribution is tight** because 57% of the answer is already locked in as actual data, leaving only ~$250K standard deviation on the remaining 3 days

resolution_criteria:
This question is a subquestion of a group question. This subquestion specifically pertains to (and resolves according to) the option 'Apr 6–12, 2026'. The resolution criteria for the parent question is below. 

These subquestions resolve as the total volume of passengers screened by the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) at its checkpoints for the rolling 7-day periods in the listed options.

TSA posts the data at the following link:
[https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes](https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes)
fine_print: This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43112) which opened on 2026-04-06 23:04:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
